title: quassia sanguinea
intro: Quassia sanguinea, also known as the blood quassia, is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the Rutaceae family and being a relative of the bitter orange. The plant's bark, roots, and leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of quassia sanguinea include relief from fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for parasites and intestinal worms. Quassia sanguinea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
